items.host_position: Agreed — the memo omits vendor lock-in as an unaddressed risk of the buy option.
items.reviewer_position: The buy option's assumptions omit the switching cost and vendor dependency.

verification.detail: The decision record's /options/1/assumptions list contains no lock-in or exit-cost entry.

items.resolution: Added a lock-in risk and a contractual exit clause to the buy option before recommending.

items.host_position: Prioritizing speed-to-market over long-term flexibility is the deliberate bet for this cycle.
items.reviewer_position: The memo under-prices the flexibility it gives up; a slower in-house build preserves optionality worth more over three years.

escalation.host_position: Speed-to-market is the priority this cycle; we accept reduced flexibility.
escalation.reviewer_position: Optionality has material long-term value the memo does not quantify.
escalation.operator_question: Is near-term speed-to-market worth the reduced long-term flexibility here, or should we preserve optionality by building in-house?
